description: End-to-end and routing-contract evals for the scope-tier-memory-check system (hook + Layer 2 rule). Eight end-to-end evals exercise the full hook-to-model pipeline via scratch_decoy (settings.local.json + MEMORY.md seeded into scratch cwd); two routing-contract evals inject synthetic system-reminders via additional_context to test Layer 2 in isolation. Issue #332.
_implementation_note: End-to-end evals use scratch_decoy instead of setup: the eval substrate seeds .claude/settings.local.json (hook registration) and .claude/projects/.../MEMORY.md (fixture memory) into the scratch tmpdir before claude spawns. This avoids setup running in the repo cwd (which would pollute the worktree). Hook path is hardcoded to the worktree absolute path (Strategy B from task spec). git-working-tree-large criterion 6 is not testable end-to-end via this architecture (git repo state cannot be seeded via scratch_decoy); it is covered by tests/hooks/scope-tier-memory-check.test.sh instead and documented as such.

evals.name: pr-330-canonical
evals.summary: Canonical positive: PR #330-shape prompt with scope-tier memory loaded and hook installed via scratch_decoy. Hook injects SCOPE-TIER MATCH reminder. Model should ack and route to direct implementation without invoking DTP. Fixture: tests/fixtures/scope-tier-memory-check/pr-330-canonical.
evals.prompt: prune the redundant per-gate floor blocks from the five delegate rules in `rules/planning.md` — they restate the DTP anchor and add no eval-measurable load

# Memory Index

- [Right-size pipeline ceremony to feature size](feedback_right_size_ceremony.md) — Small/mechanical changes (~150 LOC, single-file, known approach) should skip DTP/SA/brainstorm/FMS + subagent-driven-dev; reserve for ambiguous or high-blast-radius work

assertions.description: Structural: model emits the required ack line when SCOPE-TIER MATCH reminder is received. Memory name in the ack reflects the hook's resolved display-name ('Right-size pipeline ceremony to feature size'). Failure mode caught: Layer 2 rule edit drops the ack-line requirement.
Structural: DTP does NOT fire — scope-tier match routes to direct implementation, bypassing the planning pipeline. Failure mode caught: rule edit allows both scope-tier ack AND DTP invocation.

Structural: DTP does NOT fire when scope-tier match succeeds (no git-working-tree rejection in scratch cwd). Confirms mechanical rename prompt routes to direct implementation on match.

evals.name: routing-contract-positive
evals.summary: Routing-contract (additional_context): synthetic SCOPE-TIER MATCH reminder injected directly. Fixture: tests/fixtures/scope-tier-memory-check/routing-contract-positive. Tests Layer 2 rule in isolation from Layer 1 hook. Model should ack and route to direct implementation.
evals.additional_context: SCOPE-TIER MATCH: feedback_right_size_ceremony. Per stored feedback, this prompt qualifies as small/mechanical/known-approach. Route to direct implementation: skip DTP, Systems Analysis, brainstorming, Fat Marker Sketch, and subagent-driven-development. execution-mode.md single-implementer mode, goal-driven.md per-step verify checks, and verification.md end-of-work gate STILL apply. If this match is wrong, the user can re-prompt with explicit pipeline-invocation language.

assertions.description: Structural: model emits the required ack line when synthetic SCOPE-TIER MATCH reminder is present. The additional_context specifies 'feedback_right_size_ceremony' as the memory name. Tests Layer 2 rule text in isolation — no hook in path. Failure mode caught: rule text edit drops ack requirement.
Structural: DTP does NOT fire when synthetic SCOPE-TIER MATCH reminder is present. Tests Layer 2 routing isolation. Failure mode caught: model acks scope-tier but also fires DTP.

evals.name: hook-not-installed
evals.summary: Degradation: no hook installed, no additional_context. Fixture: tests/fixtures/scope-tier-memory-check/hook-not-installed. PR #330-shape prompt with no reminder reaching the model. Documents that Layer 2 rule text alone does NOT force routing — graceful degradation.
evals.prompt: prune the redundant per-gate floor blocks from the five delegate rules in `rules/planning.md` — they restate the DTP anchor and add no eval-measurable load

assertions.description: Structural: no reminder, no ack. Hook not installed — no SCOPE-TIER MATCH system-reminder fires; Layer 2 rule text alone does not force ack emission. Documents graceful degradation. Failure mode caught: Layer 2 rule becomes too aggressive and emits ack without a real reminder.

evals.name: routing-contract-conflict-challenge
evals.summary: Routing-contract conflict: synthetic SCOPE-TIER MATCH reminder injected, but prompt describes obviously large work ('rename Foo across public SDK — breaks all consumers'). Fixture: tests/fixtures/scope-tier-memory-check/routing-contract-conflict-challenge. Model should challenge the match rather than silently routing to direct implementation.
evals.prompt: rename `Foo` to `Bar` across the public SDK — this will break all downstream consumers
evals.additional_context: SCOPE-TIER MATCH: feedback_right_size_ceremony. Per stored feedback, this prompt qualifies as small/mechanical/known-approach. Route to direct implementation: skip DTP, Systems Analysis, brainstorming, Fat Marker Sketch, and subagent-driven-development. execution-mode.md single-implementer mode, goal-driven.md per-step verify checks, and verification.md end-of-work gate STILL apply. If this match is wrong, the user can re-prompt with explicit pipeline-invocation language.

assertions.pattern: (incorrectly|wrong signal|confirm direct implementation|hook fired|match.*wrong|incorrect match|needs pipeline|requires pipeline|break.*consumer|breaking change|not.*small|not.*mechanical)
assertions.description: Structural: model challenges the match — names why the SCOPE-TIER MATCH signal conflicts with the prompt's obvious large-scope nature. Rule text says: 'name the specific signal that's wrong and ask the user.' Failure mode caught: model silently routes to direct implementation despite the conflict.

[diagnostic: model variance on what constitutes a 'challenge' vs a nominal ack with caveats] Model does NOT silently ack + route — the challenge path diverges from the nominal routing path. Failure mode: model acks 'Routing to direct implementation' unconditionally even for obviously-wrong match.
